scenario: A process instance has an active token at Service Task T5. The team realises the customer needs an additional review step they hadn't modelled -- they want to add a parallel branch to the running instance (without modifying the BPMN definition or cancelling the instance). They want to inject a new token at task T-Review for this specific instance.
question: Can Operate's Modify Process Instance feature add new tokens at specific locations?

options.text: Operate's Modify Process Instance feature only supports cancelling active element instances -- adding new element instances or moving tokens to different positions in the process has limited documented support -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Operate's Modify Process Instance capability supports both cancelling active element instances and adding new element instances at specified positions in the process. This enables operators to skip failed activities, re-run completed activities, move tokens past blocked gateways, or advance stuck instances to correct positions without restarting the entire process instance.

Yes -- Operate's "Modify Process Instance" feature supports two main actions: (1) cancel existing tokens at specified flow nodes; (2) add new tokens at specified flow nodes (with optional variable updates for that scope). The change applies only to the targeted instance, not to the BPMN definition.

Yes, variable injection is possible but only by first modifying the BPMN process definition to add a new variable initialisation step, then redeploying the definition, so all existing instances pick up the new variable -- this describes the wrong scope entirely. Modify Instance is a per-instance operation that affects only the specific selected process instance. Modifying the BPMN definition changes future instances. The Operate Modify Instance feature directly adds or updates variables on a specific running instance without affecting the process definition or any other instances.

Modifying a running process instance's variables or execution state from Operate has no documented pathway -- all process instances run exactly as defined in the BPMN with no ability for operator intervention -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Operate provides the Modify Process Instance feature specifically for operator intervention in running instances. Operators can add variable values, update existing variables, cancel active element instances, and add new element instances at specified positions. This operational flexibility enables incident resolution, data correction, and process recovery without requiring process restart.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate's Modify Process Instance is a surgical instance-editing tool. Capabilities: Cancel tokens at specific flow nodes -- useful when an instance is stuck or routed wrong. Add tokens at specific flow nodes -- useful for adding work that wasn't originally modelled, or for skipping ahead to a downstream task. Update scope variables at the added-token's scope -- for setting up data the new token expects. Use cases: one-off interventions for specific cases (the BPMN definition is fine in general; this instance needs special handling); recovery from incidents; testing flows in production-like scenarios. Important: Modify Instance is per-instance; doesn't change the BPMN definition (other instances continue normally). Use sparingly -- frequent need for modify might indicate BPMN model gaps.
